bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My most important issue is issueE (0.3 weight), followed by issueB and issueD (0.2 each), then issueA and issueC (0.1 each). For all issues, valueE gives the highest utility (1.0), so I'll bid all valueE to maximize my utility at the start of the negotiation.

We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay close to my ideal position but show slight movement. The opponent's offer gives me very low utility: issueA:valueA(0.20), issueB:valueA(0.20), issueC:valueB(0.73), issueD:valueA(0.42), issueE:valueB(0.31) = 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.42 + 0.3*0.31 ≈ 0.02+0.04+0.073+0.084+0.093 = 0.31. Their message is aggressive but it's cheap talk. I'll concede slightly on low-importance issues (issueA, issueC) while holding firm on my high-importance issues (issueE, issueD, issueB). I'll move issueA from valueE to valueD and issueC from valueE to valueD to show good faith while keeping the most important issues at top values.

We're very early (t=0.04), so I can still hold firm on my most important issues. The opponent is bidding very aggressively (all A/B values which are low utility for me) and their rhetoric is aggressive but that's cheap talk. My most important issue is issueE (0.3 weight), followed by issueB and issueD (0.2 each). I should concede a bit more on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueC) while holding firm on issueE and partially on issueB/issueD. Let me offer a slight concession compared to my last bid on the lower-priority issues.

The opponent is repeatedly bidding {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA, issueE: valueA/valueB} — essentially their best offer, showing very little concession. Their messages are aggressive but the bids haven't moved much either. We're only at t=0.06, so there's no rush to make major concessions. My most important issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2). I'll hold firm on issueE and issueD (my highest weight issues) while making a small additional concession on issueA and issueC (lower weights). I'll slightly soften issueB from valueE to valueD to show movement while keeping high utility.

The opponent has been consistently offering the same bid (valueA on most issues, valueB on issueC and issueE) which gives me very low utility (~0.27). Their aggressive messages are cheap talk, but I should note they're not conceding at all. We're only at t=0.08, so there's plenty of time. I'll make a moderate concession on issueA and issueC (low importance to me) while holding firm on issueE (highest weight) and issueD (high weight). I'll try to vary from my last bid slightly.

The opponent has been consistently bidding very low utility for me (valueA on most issues), barely changing. Their latest bid at t=0.10 shows slight movement on issueD (valueC instead of valueA) and issueE (valueB instead of valueA), but still quite far from my ideal. Their aggressive messages are cheap talk - the actual bids show they're slowly conceding on issueD and issueE. At t=0.10, I'm still early in the negotiation, so I can hold relatively firm. My highest-weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2). I should maintain high values on those while showing some movement on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueC). I'll keep issueE at valueE, keep issueB high (valueD), keep issueD high (valueD), and make modest concessions on issueA and issueC.

The opponent is being aggressive but their actual bids have barely moved - they've been consistently pushing valueA on most issues and only slightly shifted issueD from valueA to valueC and issueE from valueA to valueB in the last couple rounds. This is cheap talk/pressure tactics. We're still very early (t=0.12), so I don't need to make large concessions yet. My highest weight issues are issueE (0.3) and issueB/issueD (0.2 each). I'll hold firm on issueE (valueE) and issueD (valueD), and make a small additional concession on lower-weight issues. I'll slightly adjust issueB downward to valueC and keep issueA at valueA to signal some flexibility.

The opponent is stuck repeating nearly the same bid (valueA on most issues, valueB on issueC and issueE) which gives me very low utility. Their aggressive messages are cheap talk. At t=0.14, it's still early - I should continue making moderate concessions on low-weight issues while holding firm on issueE (weight 0.3) and issueD (weight 0.2). My last bid was issueA:valueA, issueB:valueC, issueC:valueD, issueD:valueD, issueE:valueE. I'll try conceding issueD slightly to valueC (opponent seems to accept valueC there based on t=0.10 offer) while keeping issueE at valueE and making small adjustments elsewhere.

The opponent has been extremely consistent, essentially repeating the same bid (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA/valueC, issueE: valueA/valueB) with aggressive messaging. Their pattern shows they slightly moved issueD from valueA to valueC (a minor concession to me) and issueE from valueA to valueB. We're only at t=0.16, so there's plenty of time. My highest weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB and issueD (0.2 each). I should hold firm on issueE (valueE or valueD) and issueD (valueD or valueC), while being willing to concede on lower-weight issues. My last offer was issueA: valueA, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueC, issueE: valueD — utility = 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.56 + 0.1*0.64 + 0.2*0.74 + 0.3*0.91 ≈ 0.02+0.112+0.064+0.148+0.273 = 0.617. I'll make a slight concession on issueB from valueC to valueB to show movement, but keep issueE at valueD and issueD at valueC, and concede issueC to valueB to match their preference there.

The opponent has been extremely rigid, essentially repeating {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA/valueC, issueE: valueA/valueB} throughout. Their messages are aggressive but their actual bids show very little movement. We're still early (t=0.18), so I shouldn't over-concede. My highest-weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), issueD (0.2). I should hold firm on issueE especially. The opponent seems to care about issueD and issueB (always valueA on those). I'll try conceding a bit more on issueA (low weight for me) and issueC (low weight), while keeping issueE at valueD and issueD at valueC or better. Let me calculate my utility for a potential offer: issueA: valueA (0.1×0.20=0.02), issueB: valueB (0.2×0.74=0.148), issueC: valueB (0.1×0.73=0.073), issueD: valueC (0.2×0.74=0.148), issueE: valueD (0.3×0.91=0.273) = ~0.662. This is a reasonable position still above midpoint.

The opponent is aggressively complaining but has barely moved themselves - they've mostly stuck to valueA across issues with tiny variations. Their messages are clearly aggressive tactics (cheap talk). Looking at their latest offer: issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueE, issueE: valueB. The interesting move is issueD jumped to valueE (their best for them maybe, but good for me too - valueE gives me 1.0 utility on issueD!). We're only at t=0.20, so I shouldn't concede too fast. My most important issue is issueE (0.3 weight) - I should hold firm on valueD there. I'll keep issueE at valueD and issueD at valueD (high utility for me), and make small concessions on lower-weight issues. Let me offer something slightly different from my last bid while maintaining high utility.
